measurement_note: Size is S = m*n, the total number of balances. The legal 10x10, 25x25, and 50x50 matrices make each successive row richer, so all cells contribute to authored row totals and the final maximum. The Accepted explicit scan and an independent built-in row-sum scan take O(S) time. A correct implementation that redundantly rescans each row once per account takes O(m*n^2), completes every tier, and is rejected only by scaling.
